pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'Create a new Group named Regulatory Auditors'. Verified against Django auth.models.Group model naming conventions.

Verified from Aerobridge GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge/master/registry/models.py. Aircraft model exists in registry app. Django default permission is 'view_aircraft' (codename for Aircraft model).

Verified from Aerobridge GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge/master/registry/models.py. Contact model exists in registry app. Django default permission is 'view_contact'.

Verified from Aerobridge GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge/master/registry/models.py. Operator model exists in registry app. Django default permission is 'view_operator'.
Verified from Aerobridge GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge/master/registry/models.py. Person model exists in registry app. Django default permission is 'view_person'.

Verified from Aerobridge GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge/master/gcs_operations/models.py. FlightOperation model exists in gcs_operations app. Model name in lowercase is 'flightoperation'. Django default permission is 'view_flightoperation'.

Verified from Aerobridge GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge/master/gcs_operations/models.py. FlightPlan model exists in gcs_operations app. Model name in lowercase is 'flightplan'. Django default permission is 'view_flightplan'.

Django permission naming convention: 'add_', 'change_', 'delete_' are standard write permission prefixes. Task requires view-only permissions, which use no prefix or 'view_' prefix. These forbidden prefixes correctly identify write operations.
Verified from installation script (install_aerobridge.sh) which clones from this repository. This is the official Aerobridge project (drone management system).
Verified from installation script (install_aerobridge.sh, lines 150-156) which creates superuser with username 'admin' and password 'adminpass123'.
Verified from installation script which confirms SQLite is the default database when POSTGRES_HOST is not set in .env configuration.

privileged_info_summary: The create_auditor_group task operates on Aerobridge, a real Django-based drone management system from https://github.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge. The task requires creating a Django group named 'Regulatory Auditors' with exactly 6 view-only permissions from two Aerobridge apps: registry (aircraft, contact, operator, person) and gcs_operations (flightoperation, flightplan). All specified permission codenames (view_aircraft, view_contact, view_operator, view_person, view_flightoperation, view_flightplan) correspond to standard Django view permissions for actual models that exist in the Aerobridge codebase. The admin login credentials are 'admin' / 'adminpass123', and the data persists in a SQLite database at /opt/aerobridge/aerobridge.sqlite3.
